exampleSentences: Em nhớ cất sách vào trong ngăn kéo sau khi đọc xong nhé.
Anh vừa cất áo khoác vào tủ khi về đến nhà.
Chị thường cất tiền ở một nơi an toàn trong phòng ngủ.
notes:
cất = to put something away, store it somewhere safe or tidy. You use it when you move something out of sight after using it.

It’s a verb, usually followed by an object (the thing you’re putting away). Often combined with a place using vào.

Structure: cất + object + vào + place (cất giày vào tủ)

It can also mean “to raise” in fixed phrases like cất tiếng (start singing or speaking). A close synonym is để (put), but cất feels more like “put away neatly/out of the way.”

example.es: Puse el libro en el armario.
example.en: I put the book in the cabinet.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Remember to put away the book in the drawer after reading.
I just put away my jacket in the closet when I got home.
She usually puts away money in a safe place in her bedroom.

translation.en: I will put the book on the shelf.

notes_md:
**cất** = to put something away, store it somewhere safe or tidy. You use it when you move something out of sight after using it.

It’s a verb, usually followed by an object (the thing you’re putting away). Often combined with a place using *vào*.

`Structure: cất + object + vào + place` (cất giày vào tủ)

It can also mean “to raise” in fixed phrases like *cất tiếng* (start singing or speaking). A close synonym is **để** (put), but **cất** feels more like “put away neatly/out of the way.”

translation.en: You put the items into the cupboard.
